title: Vercel deploy succeeds but app crashes — missing env vars
summary: Build passes because env vars aren't required at build time, but the app crashes at runtime because they're not set in Vercel's environment settings.
mistake: Deploying to Vercel without checking that all `process.env.*` variables used in the app are configured in Vercel's Environment Variables dashboard. The build succeeds because Next.js doesn't fail on missing env vars at build time (they're just `undefined`). The agent sees 'deploy successful' and reports done. The user opens the URL and gets a 500 error.
instead: Before running `vercel --prod`, run `vercel env ls` and cross-reference with every `process.env.*` in the codebase. For each missing variable, run `vercel env add <NAME>`. Also check `.env.local` and `.env.production` for any vars that exist locally but not on Vercel. Never assume 'build passed = app works'.
